zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Reed於大約1383年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾托丁頓時，他的父親Thomas Rede48歲，母親Catherine Boteler23歲。他與Elizabeth Winter結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1409年4月20日之前在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾托丁頓，埋葬在英國英格蘭奧可福爾肯登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Reed于大约1383年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾托丁頓时，他的父亲Thomas Rede48岁，母亲Catherine Boteler23岁。他与Elizabeth Winter结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1409年4月20日之前在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾托丁頓，埋葬在英国英格兰奧可福爾肯登。

nameMeanings.name: Reed
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname from Middle English and Older Scots red(e) ‘red’, no doubt denoting someone with red hair or a ruddy complexion.

English: from Middle English ride, rede, rude (Old English rīed, rēod, rȳd) ‘clearing’. The surname may be topographic for someone who lived in or near a clearing, or habitational, for someone who lived at one of a number of places so named, including Rede Court in Strood (Kent), Rides in Eastchurch (Kent), Ride Way in Ewhurst (Surrey), and Reed Farm in Wadhurst (Sussex). The word is particularly common in the southeastern counties of England, from Kent to the Isle of Wight. See also Rider and Reader .

English: habitational name from Read (Lancashire), Reed (Hertfordshire), or Rede (Suffolk). The Lancashire placename derives from Old English rǣge ‘roe, female roe deer’ + hēafod ‘head’. The Hertfordshire placename derives from Old English rȳhth ‘rough piece of ground’. The etymology of the Suffolk placename is uncertain.

variants.name: Read
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Reed  .

History: A family called Read were established in America in the early 18th century by John Read, who was born in Dublin, sixth in descent from Sir Thomas Read of Berkshire, England. His son, George Read (1733–98), was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence, and as a lawyer helped frame the Constitution.

variants.name: Ried
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Friedrich, Johann.
German: topographic name from Middle High German riet ‘damp, mossy area, wet land’ or ‘reed grass, swamp’.
German: habitational name from any of various places so named in Hesse, Baden-Württemberg, Austria, Switzerland.

variants.name: Reede
meanings: English: variant of   Reed  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 423, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

variants.name: Reeder
meanings: English: occupational name for someone who used reeds as a roofing material (a thatcher), a derivative of Middle English rede ‘reed’ + -er. Compare Reed and Redman .

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a clearing, a derivative of Middle English rede, ride, rude ‘clearing’ (see Reed ) + -er, synonymous and interchangeable with atte Rede, atte Rude ‘at the clearing’. This type of name, composed of a topographic term + -er, is common in Kent, Surrey and Sussex, and it is also found in Essex.
Dutch and German: variant of   Reder   3.

variants.name: Reeds
meanings: English: variant of Reed , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Possibly an Americanized form of German   Reetz   or   Rietz  .

variants.name: Redd
meanings: English: variant of   Reed  .

variants.name: Reid
meanings: Scottish: variant of Reed , of which Reid is the common modern Scots spelling.

History: Samuel Chester Reid (1783–1861), the sea captain who designed the stars and stripes form of the present the American flag, was born at Norwich, CT. His father, Lt. John Reid, a former British naval officer of a distinguished Glasgow family, had resigned his commission and joined the cause of the American Revolution.

variants.name: Roy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Lucien, Normand, Andre, Jacques, Marcel, Emile, Laurent, Pierre, Cecile, Donat, Gilles.  Indian Dipak, Sumit, Amit, Ajit, Dipankar, Partha, Ranjan, Shyamal, Aloke, Amitava, Chitra, Gautam.
Scottish: nickname for a person with red hair or a ruddy complexion, from Gaelic ruadh ‘red’. Compare Reed .
English (of Norman origin): from Old French roi ‘king’, used as a nickname (see 3 below) and also as a personal name. Compare Ray 2.

variants.name: Rider
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English ridere ‘horseman’, perhaps with a specific application such as ‘forest ranger’, ‘horseman who guards crops’, or ‘mounted warrior, knight’. Compare Knight .
English: habitational name from Ryther, near Selby (Yorkshire), probably from Old English ryther ‘clearing’.

English: topographic name from Middle English ridere ‘dweller at a clearing’ (see Reeder ) or ‘dweller by a small stream’, depending on which sense of Middle English ride the surname is derived from. These topographic senses of Rider are limited to the southern counties of England (especially Surrey, Sussex, and Hampshire), where topographic names in -er and the Middle English words ride ‘clearing’ and rithe ‘small stream’ are mainly found. See also Reed .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Reed was born about 1383, in Toddington, Gloucestershire, England, his father, Thomas Rede, was 48 and his mother, Catherine Boteler, was 23. He married Elizabeth Winter. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died before 20 April 1409, in Toddington, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Checkendon, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Reed est né environ 1383, à Toddington, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Rede, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Catherine Boteler, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Winter. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé avant 20 avril 1409, à Toddington, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Checkendon, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Reed um 1383 in Toddington, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Rede 48 und seine Mutter Catherine Boteler 23. Er hat Elizabeth Winter geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb vor 20. April 1409 in Toddington, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Checkendon, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Reed è nato il circa 1383 nel Toddington, Gloucestershire, England, il suo padre, Thomas Rede, aveva 48 anni e il suo madre, Catherine Boteler, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Winter. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì prima del 20 aprile 1409, nel Toddington, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Checkendon, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Reedが約1383年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアトッドイントンで生まれた時，父Thomas Redeは48歳，母Catherine Botelerは23歳であった。Elizabeth Winterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1409年4月20日以前にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアトッドイントンで死去し，イギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアチェクエンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Reed(이)가 대략 1383년 영국 그루스스터실르 토딩턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Rede(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine Boteler(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Elizabeth Winter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1409년 4월 20일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 토딩턴에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 체c켄던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Reed nasceu aproximadamente 1383, em Toddington, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Rede, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Catherine Boteler, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Winter. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu antes 20 de abril de 1409, em Toddington, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Checkendon, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Reed родился около 1383 г. в местности тоддингтон, глоусестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Rede было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine Boteler, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Winter. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер перед 20 апрель 1409 г. в местности тоддингтон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности чеккендон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
